title: Judge
description: An LLM-graded scorer. Judges produce a structured verdict via a model call, gated by an `output_schema` so the verdict is machine-readable. Judges get their own budget node and their own calibration loop (research/12 §2.4, §3.x).

model_ref.description: Which model grades. SHOULD be pinned (`pinned_snapshot`) — judge drift across model versions is the single most common eval-validity bug.

rubric.description: Either an inline rubric string (markdown / plain text) OR a reference into the PromptStore for a versioned rubric. Inline rubrics are fingerprinted into the cache key so edits invalidate prior judgements.

rubric_ref.description: Content-addressed or named reference into the PromptStore.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema (any dialect supported by the underlying provider's structured-output mode) describing the judge's verdict object. Open by design — this is itself a schema.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for the judge. Defaults to 0 in deterministic eval mode.

calibration_ref.description: Optional reference to a stored calibration record (Cohen's κ vs. human labels). When set, the harness MAY refuse to swap the judge model if κ degrades below threshold.
